name: c-cpp-project-manager-and-build
description: Create, organize, build, run and debug generic C/C++ workspaces and projects with GCC, MinGW or another configurable toolchain.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No C/C++ workspace is currently loaded.
[Open a workspace](command:cpm.openWorkspace)
[Create a workspace and project](command:cpm.createWorkspaceProject)
[Open the home page](command:cpm.openHome)

cpm.installations.description: Known C/C++ toolchain root or bin directories detected or added manually.
cpm.installations.markdownDescription: Known C/C++ toolchain root or bin directories detected or added manually.

cpm.activeInstallation.description: Selected C/C++ toolchain root or bin directory. Leave empty to use automatic detection from PATH and common MinGW/MSYS2/Clang locations.

cpm.buildMode.description: Build mode used by the generic C/C++ build pipeline. Debug modes add -g -O0; release modes add -O2.

cpm.runArguments.description: Arguments passed to the active executable when running it.

cpm.projectFormatVersion.description: Internal .cws/.prj format version used when generating new C/C++ Project Manager workspaces. Keep the default unless you need compatibility with a specific legacy parser.

cpm.autoLoadWorkspace.description: Automatically load a .cws file when the opened VS Code folder contains exactly one C/C++ workspace.

cpm.autoConfigureCppTools.description: Automatically create or update the managed C/C++ Project Manager entry in .vscode/c_cpp_properties.json when a C/C++ workspace is opened or the selected installation changes. Disabled by default to avoid slowing Microsoft C/C++ IntelliSense; use the Sync IntelliSense command manually.

cpm.autoAddCpmFolderToWorkspace.description: Automatically add the precise C/C++ project root to the standard VS Code Explorer. Disabled by default to avoid unexpected workspace changes and large IntelliSense indexing scopes.

cpm.intelliSenseCompilerPath.description: Optional explicit compiler executable used by Microsoft C/C++ IntelliSense. Leave empty to use the selected gcc/g++/clang toolchain.

cpm.additionalIncludePaths.description: Additional include directories appended to the generated C/C++ IntelliSense configuration.

cpm.enableSupplementalCompletionProvider.description: Provide supplemental completion items parsed from active project sources only. Disabled by default to leave completion fully managed by Microsoft C/C++ and avoid extra work while typing.

cpm.enableStandardLibraryCompletionProvi.description: Provide lightweight completion items for common C/C++ standard-library symbols only when the matching header is not already included. This avoids duplicates with Microsoft C/C++ IntelliSense while still allowing auto-include insertion before the header exists.

cpm.standardLibraryCompletionAutoInclude.description: When a standard-library completion item is accepted, insert the required #include if it is missing from the current file.

cpm.showPersistentStatusBarActions.description: Show compact C/C++ Project Manager actions in the VS Code status bar. These actions remain available when the C/C++ Workspace view is collapsed.

cpm.cCompilerPath.description: C compiler executable used by the generic build pipeline, for example gcc or an absolute MinGW gcc.exe path.

cpm.cppCompilerPath.description: C++ compiler and linker executable used by the generic build pipeline, for example g++ or an absolute MinGW g++.exe path.

cpm.archiverPath.description: Static-library archiver executable used for Static Library targets.

cpm.debuggerPath.description: GDB executable used when launching the VS Code cppdbg debugger.

cpm.outputDirectory.description: Directory, relative to the project file by default, used to store generic compiler objects and fallback targets.

cpm.cStandard.description: C language standard passed as -std=<value>. auto does not pass a C standard flag.

cpm.cppStandard.description: C++ language standard passed as -std=<value>. auto does not pass a C++ standard flag.

cpm.warningLevel.description: Predefined warning profile used by the generic GCC/MinGW build pipeline. none passes no warning flag; all adds -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wconversion.

cpm.optimizationLevel.description: Optimization profile used by the generic build pipeline. mode-default uses -O0 for Debug and -O2 for Release.

cpm.debugInformation.description: Debug-information profile used by the generic build pipeline. mode-default adds -g only for Debug configurations.

cpm.architectureMode.description: Architecture flag mode. auto uses the selected compiler default; from-build-mode maps Debug/Release to -m32 and Debug64/Release64 to -m64.

cpm.compilerFlags.description: Additional flags passed to both C and C++ compilation commands.

cpm.cCompilerFlags.description: Additional flags passed only to C compilation commands.

cpm.cppCompilerFlags.description: Additional flags passed only to C++ compilation commands.

cpm.linkerFlags.description: Additional flags passed to the final link command.

cpm.includePaths.description: Additional include directories. Relative paths are resolved from the project file directory.

cpm.libraryPaths.description: Additional library search directories passed as -L entries. Relative paths are resolved from the project file directory.

cpm.libraries.description: Additional libraries passed to the linker. Values may be names such as m/ws2_32 or complete -l flags.

cpm.useBuildModeArchitectureFlags.description: When enabled, x86 modes add -m32 and x64 modes add -m64. Keep disabled if you select architecture by choosing a specific compiler path.

cpm.deployRuntimeDlls.description: Deploy MinGW runtime DLLs such as libgcc_s_*.dll, libstdc++-6.dll and libwinpthread-1.dll beside generated executables/DLLs. auto copies only DLLs found in the selected toolchain bin directory.

cpm.useLocalBuildCacheForOneDrive.description: When the project is located under OneDrive and the normal object directory cannot be created, fall back to a local build cache for intermediate object files.

cpm.cleanRuntimeDllsOnDeploy.description: Before deploying MinGW runtime DLLs beside the target, remove stale or architecture-mismatched MinGW runtime DLLs from the target directory. This prevents x86/x64 runtime DLL mix-ups when switching build modes.
